fix(repo): step through npm@10 before upgrading to npm@latest in deploy workflow (#56)

Directly installing `npm@latest` (npm 11) from older npm versions
triggers a known upgrade bug. The deploy workflow must first land on npm
10 before jumping to 11.

## Changes

- **`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`**: Split the single `npm install -g
npm@latest` step into two sequential installs:
  ```yaml
  run: |
    npm install -g npm@10
    npm install -g npm@latest
  ```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: AgentEnder <6933928+AgentEnder@users.noreply.github.com>

Import full Puritan Library (7117 works, 1053 authors)

commit.message:
Add missing multi-byte command handlers to SDL emulators

Previously, unhandled multi-byte commands (e.g. 0xD5 Clock Divide on
SSD1306) would cause their argument bytes to be misinterpreted as new
commands, potentially corrupting emulator state.

SSD1306: Add 0x20, 0x8D, 0xD5, 0xD9, 0xDA, 0xDB (1-arg), 0x26/0x27
  (6-arg scroll), 0x29/0x2A (5-arg scroll)
SSD1331: Add 0x81-0x83, 0x87, 0x8A-0x8C, 0xB1, 0xB3, 0xB6, 0xBB,
  0xBE, 0x26 (1-arg), 0x22 (10-arg rect), 0x24/0x25 (4-arg),
  0x27 (5-arg scroll)
SSD1351: Add 0xA1, 0xA2, 0xAB, 0xB1, 0xB3, 0xB5, 0xB6, 0xBB, 0xBE,
  0xC7, 0xCA, 0xFD (1-arg), 0xC1 (3-arg contrast)
ILI9341: Add 0x26, 0x3A, 0x51, 0x53, 0x55, 0xC7, 0xF6 (1-arg),
  0xB1, 0xB5, 0xB6, 0xC0, 0xC1, 0xC5 (2-arg), 0xCF (3-arg),
  0xCB (5-arg), 0xE0/0xE1 (15-arg gamma)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix segfault on Ctrl+C in SDL emulator demos

Three issues caused the crash:

1. sdl_graphics_close() only destroyed the SDL window, leaking the
   renderer, texture, and pixel buffer. Now properly destroys all
   resources in reverse creation order with NULL guards.

2. SDL_QUIT event handler called exit(0) without cleanup. Now calls
   sdl_core_close() before exiting to properly release SDL resources.

3. No signal handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM. Ctrl+C would terminate the
   process mid-render, causing use-after-free in SDL cleanup. Added
   sdl_signal_handler that calls sdl_core_close() then _exit(0).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix GPT-5.4 + Goldeneye review findings (6 issues)

1. Missing icons: Add LayoutList, Wrench, Coins, FileEdit, Square to Icon.jsx
2. History not sent: Use messagesRef to read history synchronously (was
   relying on React state updater side effect, which runs asynchronously)
3. Bold turn refs not clickable: Remove bold wrapping from longest answer
   so [Turn N] renders as a clickable ref button
4. Session leak on error: Always disconnect() before nulling _persistentSession
5. Session context bleeding: Add /api/qa/reset endpoint, called by clear()
   to invalidate server-side persistent session
6. Regex false positives: Move 'longest' pattern first, tighten which/tools
   and which/files to require verb (were/was/did), prevent 'which tool took
   the most time?' from matching tools instead of falling through to model

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(skills): improve AI discoverability routing (#9)

Skill discoverability is weakened by AGENTS.md drift,
malformed skill frontmatter, redundant content across
skills, and unclear skill boundaries. This makes it
harder for AI agents to route to the correct skill.

- Fix AGENTS.md inventory drift (remove stale skills,
  add missing ones)
- Normalize frontmatter across 8 skill manifests (SPDX
  placement, multi-line descriptions, heading structure)
- Consolidate redundant categorical tables into a single
  Discoverability Index with positive Scope/Activate
  When/Signals columns
- Restore shell-script as a dedicated skill with POSIX,
  Bash, and Zsh coverage
- Document optional allowed-tools support per agentskills
  spec, with guidance to prefer specific tools over bash
- De-bloat pull-request skill by replacing duplicated
  content (commit format, PR body, self-review, branch
  fetch) with links to owning skills
- Add platform boundary notes: pull-request is platform-
  agnostic, github is GitHub-specific
- Simplify general-programming testing refs to point to
  testing skill without duplicating philosophy
- Tighten verbose frontmatter descriptions (java,
  pull-request)
- Trim AGENTS.md sections that duplicated language-skill
  content (code style, testing philosophy, skill creation
  workflow)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 4: Rich markdown rendering, clickable turn refs, new tests

QADrawer.jsx:
- Enhanced parseMessageContent with support for code blocks, inline code,
  headers (h1-h3), list items (bullet points), and newlines
- New renderParts function handles all part types with themed styles
- parseInline uses a single combined regex for turn refs + bold + code

qaClassifier.js:
- Turn detail answers now use plain [Turn N] format (not bold-wrapped)
  so the parser correctly produces clickable ref buttons

qaDrawer.test.jsx:
- Added tests: inline code rendering, Files/Longest insights visibility,
  turn ref clickable buttons

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 3: Persistent SDK session, conversation history, batched tokens

qaAgent.js:
- Persistent SDK session across Q&A calls (no more create/disconnect per question)
- Session auto-invalidated on error, recreated on next call
- Conversation history injected into prompt for follow-up context
- Guard against duplicate content.delta + assistant.message output
- Stream status events (onStatus callback) for richer UI feedback

useQA.js:
- Send conversation history (last 10 messages) to server
- 50ms token batching to reduce React re-renders during streaming
- Handle server-side status events and update streamingStatus

routes/ai.js:
- Stream initial 'Analyzing session...' status event before model call
- Forward onStatus events from qaAgent to SSE stream

commit.message:
Add SSD1325 and SH1107 advanced emulator tests (338 tests)

SSD1325 tests (ssd1325_advanced_tests.cpp):
- copyBlock command (0x23): forward copy, backward copy, overlapping regions
- Horizontal addressing mode (s_verticalMode=0): fillRect, area fill
- COM split and remap modes: comSplit match/mismatch, right-to-left,
  bottom-to-top, nibble remap flag
- Additional commands: contrast, display on/off, multiplex ratio,
  bulk config commands, unknown command fallthrough
- SSD1327 COM split scenarios: default, disabled (remap), enabled (no remap)

SH1107 tests (sh1107_advanced_tests.cpp):
- Column addressing mode (0x21): sets pageAddressingMode=0
- Display all-white mode (0xA5): displayMode |= 2
- Display offset (0xD3), start line (0xDC), COM scan direction (0xC0/0xC8)
- flipHorizontal, contrast, display on/off, misc config commands
- Raw page/column selection via 0xBn/0x0n/0x1n commands

Coverage: sdl_ssd1325.c 68.7% -> 93.9%, sdl_sh1107.c 84.7% -> 97.8%.
Overall: 84.8% -> 86.4% lines, 89.7% -> 90.4% functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Redesign Q&A drawer: Quick insights, stop button, lifted state

Phase 1 of Q&A improvement plan:

- Replace SuggestedChips with Quick insights panel (Summary, Tools,
  Errors, Cost buttons with icon labels instead of fake chat questions)
- Add Stop button wired to qa.abort() during streaming
- Lift useQA hook from QADrawer to AppSessionView so conversation
  state persists across drawer open/close
- Add multi-phase streaming status (Analyzing session / Generating answer)
- Add unique message IDs instead of array index keys
- Show 'quick answer' vs 'AI answer' badge on response bubbles
- Fix frontend timeout to cancel the reader on timeout (was fire-and-forget)
- Align frontend timeout to 60s to match backend SESSION_TIMEOUT_MS
- Fix abort() to preserve partial streamed content
- Reuse classifier context instead of rebuilding it
- Fix getUserMessages() to return last N messages not first N
- Update QADrawer tests for new prop interface and Quick insights UI
- Add test for quick answer badge

commit.message:
Add rotation and TFT variant tests, fix IL9163 emulator state leak

Add 36 new tests covering IL9163/ILI9341/SSD1331 rotation modes, ST7735
80x160 and 128x160 variants, and ST7789 135x240 variant in
rotation_tests.cpp.

Fix IL9163 emulator reset function to clear all state (colConnection,
rowConnection, TOTAL_COLUMNS, TOTAL_ROWS, verticalMode, column/page
state) preventing cross-test contamination when different TFT display
types share the same emulator code.

Coverage: sdl_il9163.c 62.8% -> 96.8%, sdl_ili9341.c 78.4% -> 96.4%.
Overall: 83.0% -> 84.8% lines (301 tests, 0 valgrind errors).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix SDL emulator state leaks between tests

- Add reset() functions to ILI9341, SSD1325, SSD1351, PCD8544 emulators
  to clear stale 'detected' flags between test runs
- Call unregister_oleds() at start of sdl_core_init() to prevent
  p_oled_db array from growing on repeated init calls
- Move ILI9341 s_firstStart from function-local to file-scope static
  so reset() can reach it
- Zero g_pixels buffer after malloc in sdl_graphics_set_oled_params()
  to prevent uninitialized memory reads
- Fix SH1107 emulator: multiplexRatio check >= to > (off-by-one
  disabled last display row)

Root cause: stale 'detected=1' flags in emulators without reset()
caused wrong display driver to win auto-detection when tests ran
in sequence, leading to buffer overflows (320x240 ILI9341 params
applied to 128x128 IL9163 buffer).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
